water.name: Nesbitt Lake
aliases: Nesbitt Lake (Key Pittman Wildlife Management Area)

authorities.name: U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons and Regulations CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Nesbitt Lake (Key Pittman Wildlife Management Area): Open year around except the portion above the old fence line as posted is closed to trespass February 15 through August 15 and closed to fishing during the waterfowl season. Daily: 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass. No limit on bullheads (unlimited possession). Possession: 30 warmwater game fish of which not more than 20 may be black bass. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Nesbitt Lake — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: The sport fishery consists of black bullhead, largemouth bass, white crappie, and bluegill, and fathead minnows serve as an additional prey base for bass.

sources.title: Key Pittman Wildlife Management Area — Travel Nevada
Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Reservoirs layer record for gaz_id 845919, "Nesbitt Lake", Reservoir, NV, Lincoln Co.
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":845919,"gaz_name":"Nesbitt Lake","gaz_featureclass":"Reservoir","state_alpha":"NV","county_name":"Lincoln"} — geometry (Web Mercator -12826701.8631, 4519040.0851) converts to lon -115.22422, lat 37.57130, matching the file's recorded coordinate (-115.22420, 37.57130) to within ~5m.

regulations.humanSummary: Nesbitt Lake is open to fishing year-round, except for a closed portion above the old fence line (see closure rule).
regulations.verbatimText: Open year around except the portion above the old fence line as posted is closed to trespass February 15 through August 15 and closed to fishing during the waterfowl season.

parameters.note: The portion of Nesbitt Lake above the old fence line, as posted, is closed to trespass (and therefore fishing) from February 15 through August 15 each year, and is also closed to fishing during the waterfowl hunting season.

regulations.humanSummary: The upper portion of Nesbitt Lake above the old posted fence line is closed to trespass (and fishing) Feb 15–Aug 15, and closed to fishing during the waterfowl season.
regulations.verbatimText: the portion above the old fence line as posted is closed to trespass February 15 through August 15 and closed to fishing during the waterfowl season

parameters.counting_rule: Of the 15 daily warmwater game fish, not more than 10 may be black bass. Of the 30 in possession, not more than 20 may be black bass.

regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 15 warmwater game fish (up to 10 black bass); possession limit 30 warmwater game fish (up to 20 black bass) at Nesbitt Lake.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass. Possession: 30 warmwater game fish of which not more than 20 may be black bass.

parameters.counting_rule: No limit on bullheads; unlimited daily catch and unlimited possession.

regulations.humanSummary: No bag or possession limit on bullheads at Nesbitt Lake.

parameters.note: No time-of-day restriction, except portion of the lake closed above the old fence line during posted dates and waterfowl season.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is allowed any hour of the day or night.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
